concepts.name: The Indoor Weather Service
concepts.premise: Treat ambient indoor WiFi as a delicate atmospheric microclimate where every human movement causes measurable radio pressure shifts.
concepts.hook: Right now your living room is filled with an invisible atmosphere of radio waves, and every heartbeat causes a storm.
concepts.metaphor: Micro-meteorology of ambient radio frequency turbulence
concepts.motif: Isobaric RF wave contour maps deforming around unseen human silhouettes in real time
sections: Reading Body Movement as RF Turbulence
concepts.why_it_fits: WiFi Channel State Information behaves identically to atmospheric wave scattering, turning routine RF noise into predictive room telemetry.

concepts.name: Dark Sector Controller
concepts.premise: Reframe smart home presence as air traffic control tracking non-cooperative targets through zero-visibility blackout conditions.
concepts.hook: Air traffic control tracks airliners through solid cloud cover without cameras; this eight dollar chip does the exact same thing for your bedroom.
concepts.metaphor: Dark-sector passive radar tactical command center
concepts.motif: Polar radar scope sweeps tagging human targets with live cardiac telemetry callouts
concepts.why_it_fits: RuView generates spatial vector tracking and vital sign telemetry purely from commodity hardware echo patterns without optical sight.

concepts.name: The 55KB Shadow Reporter
concepts.premise: Expose how a minuscule Rust binary hijacks existing household router chatter to log human existence straight to Home Assistant.
concepts.hook: What if I told you an eight dollar chip running fifty-five kilobytes of Rust can read your pulse through a closed door?
concepts.metaphor: Embedded low-footprint wire reporter logging physical events
concepts.motif: Live binary CSI telemetry streams rendering directly into clean Home Assistant event logs
sections: Deploying the Rust Wire to Home Assistant
concepts.why_it_fits: Highlights RuView's radical efficiency in compressing complex Hugging Face spatial models down to bare-metal microcontroller memory footprints.
